commodities.name: pitahayas
terms: commercial species and varieties of pitahaya
pitahaya from genera selenicereus and hylocereus

sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to commercial species and varieties of pitahayas grown from the genera Selenicereus and Hylocereus, of the Cactaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Pitahayas for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to commercial species and varieties of pitahayas grown from the genera Selenicereus and Hylocereus, of the Cactaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Pitahayas for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to commercial species and varieties of pitahayas grown from the genera Selenicereus and Hylocereus, of the Cactaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Pitahayas for industrial processing are excluded.

Classification

sections.title: The pitahayas must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness2 account being taken of the characteristics of the variety and/or commercial type and the area in which they are grown.
path_titles: The pitahayas must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness2 account being taken of the characteristics of the variety and/or commercial type and the area in which they are grown.
sections.text:
The development and condition of the pitahayas must be such as to enable them:
­ to withstand transport and handling; and
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Pitahayas in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > Classification > “Extra” Class
number=2.2.1
level=3
Pitahayas in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text: Pitahayas in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.text:
Pitahayas in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package:
- slight defects in shape;
- slight defects of the skin not exceeding 1 cm2 of the total surface area of the fruit.
The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

sections.text:

B 151 – 200 151 – 200
C 201 – 260 201 – 250
D 261 – 360 251 – 300
E > 361 301 – 400
F ------ 401 – 500
G ------ 501 – 600
H ------ 601 – 700
I ------ > 701

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
number=4
level=1
Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

page_spans.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

sections.title: Class II
sections.text:
This class includes pitahayas which do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the pitahayas retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation:
- defects in shape;
- defects of the skin not exceeding 2 cm2 of the total surface area of the fruit.
The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).
sections.retrieval_text:
CONTAMINANTS
number=7
level=1
The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).

page_spans.text: The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).

sections.title: Uniformity
sections.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only pitahayas of the same origin, variety and/or commercial type, quality, colour and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION > Uniformity

page_spans.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only pitahayas of the same origin, variety and/or commercial type, quality, colour and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: Packaging
sections.text: Pitahayas must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new4, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Pitahayas shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 44-1995).

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the pitahayas. Packages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sentences.original: This Standard applies to commercial species and varieties of pitahayas grown from the genera Selenicereus and Hylocereus, of the Cactaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging.

classification.sentence: Pitahayas for industrial processing are excluded.
classification.pattern: Simple declarative sentence with exclusion clause
classification.justification: Expresses exclusion which functions as prohibition but with a unique straightforward form without known template match.
notes: Exclusion often treated as prohibition but structurally unique.

classification.pattern: Complex sentence with introductory conditional phrase followed by bulleted mandatory attributes

components.condition: In all classes, subject to the special provisions for each class and the tolerances allowed
obligations: must be sound (excluding rotting or deterioration)
must be clean and free of visible foreign matter
must be practically free of pests affecting appearance and damage
must be free of abnormal external moisture (excluding condensation)
must be free of any foreign smell and/or taste
must be free of cracks in the skin
must have peduncle or stem between 15 and 25 mm

classification.sentence: They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.
classification.pattern: [Main Obligation] + [Exception introduced by 'with the exception of'] + [Condition introduced by 'provided that']

components.obligation: They must be free of defects
components.exception: with the exception of very slight superficial defects
components.condition: provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package

classification.justification: The sentence expresses a normative obligation to be defect-free but includes a specific exception and a condition qualifying that exception. This composite structure with both exception and conditional qualification does not strongly match any known templates, leading to a new pattern classification with high confidence in modality and function.
notes: The sentence combines an obligation with both an exception and a condition, which is a complex normative structure absent from the current template catalog.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR PITAHAYAS
pages.summary: Página contendo apenas o título principal do documento e cabeçalho com código; sem seções ou texto estruturado para mapeamento.

A página apresenta dois títulos de nível 1: 'DEFINITION OF PRODUCE' com texto explicativo e 'PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY' que é subdividido em subseções numeradas de níveis 2 e 3 detalhando requisitos mínimos e classificação do produto.

A página apresenta a continuidade da seção 2.2.3 Class II com conteúdo sobre defeitos permitidos, seguida pelos títulos de nível 1 'PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING' e 'PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES'. Esta última contém subtítulos de níveis 2 e 3 que detalham tolerâncias de qualidade e tamanho. O conteúdo está organizado hierarquicamente com associação clara de textos a cada título. A seção 2.2.3 indica continuidade da página anterior.

A página apresenta a continuação das seções principais 5, 6 e 7, com suas subseções e subsubseções bem hierarquizadas. O título do documento não está visível, o cabeçalho da página está presente. Os textos estão associados corretamente a seus respectivos títulos, sem sinais de continuidade entre páginas neste conteúdo.

A página contém seções estruturadas com numeração hierárquica clara: seção 7.2, que continua da página anterior, e seção 8 com subseções 8.1 e 8.2. O cabeçalho da página está presente. As seções 8.1 e 8.2 foram identificadas como subtítulos da seção 8, com seus respectivos textos incorporados aos títulos por falta de texto adicional separado. Não há indicação de continuação para a próxima página.
